description: PUBLIC completeness contract for a delegated OKRA handoff-contract artifact. Derived entirely from the skill's own published Output section and the documented case requirements. This is the product's own definition-of-done. It intentionally checks presence/structure of documented sections and fields, NOT the hidden eval rubric: it contains no regex adjacency rules, no scoring, no case-specific phrasing traps, and imports nothing from evals/blindbox/checks. The hidden checkers stay independent and grade honest application.
source: skills/reverse-tornado-okr/SKILL.md (Output section for delegated loops) + evals/blindbox/cases/okra-handoff-contracts.json prompt. Every requirement below cites a public source.
match: case-insensitive substring over whitespace-collapsed text; multi-word tokens tolerate line wrapping.
coherence_review: Every PKR must explicitly carry ALL of: linked_ckr, source_dkr_checkpoint, contribution_metric, a done check, progress signals, and a hand-back rule. Never write that a PKR may omit, need not carry, or lacks any of these.
CKR must be described as measurable contribution context, never as worker work and never dispatched or executed as a task.
The DKR-to-DKR handoff's risk_or_anti_goal_implications must contain real content; never leave it none / empty / n/a / tbd.
The in-progress influence rule must say worker narrative is NOT evidence; never say narrative may or can influence the next dispatch.
The orchestrator must keep steering until the objective metric reaches target; never say the loop stops when the board / PKR list / worker queue is complete.
Every CKR must show its own discovery/delivery balance (a mini reverse-tornado), not just a metric.

Every candidate anti-goal in the guardrail library must carry all its documented fields (metric_id, threshold, type, applies_when, does_not_apply_when, invalidates_when or recertify_by, source_refs, trace evidence, candidate_status, no_regression_evidence). Never say a candidate anti-goal may omit, need not include, or omits any of these fields.
coherence_review_source: Each rule restates a documented requirement from skills/reverse-tornado-okr/SKILL.md (Steps 2, 2b, 3, 6, Output). These are positive product rules for self-review, not the hidden eval's detection patterns.

requirements.description: Worker prompt packet contract names all documented packet keys.
requirements.hint: Add a 'Worker prompt packet contract' section listing every key including output_schema.

requirements.description: In-progress influence rule includes the exact published sentence.

tokens: In-progress worker narrative is not evidence; only worker progress, check-ins, metric reads, flags, or accepted checkpoints can influence the next dispatch.

requirements.hint: Include the exact in-progress influence sentence in its own section.

requirements.description: DKR-to-DKR handoff names all documented fields.
requirements.hint: DKR-to-DKR handoff section must name all nine fields.

requirements.description: DKR-to-DKR handoff includes a concrete worked instantiation, not only a field table.
requirements.hint: Write one concrete worked instantiation: name the previous DKR learning checkpoint, its decision target, a confidence/probability update before and after (prior -> posterior), and the orchestrator decision (accepted/held/rejected).
requirements.source: SKILL.md 'the golden worked example' pattern; case prompt worked-instantiation requirement

requirements.description: Every PKR carries CKR-to-PKR trace fields.
requirements.hint: Each PKR must carry linked_ckr, source_dkr_checkpoint, and contribution_metric.

requirements.description: Artifact states CKR is measurable contribution context, not worker work.
requirements.hint: State explicitly that CKR is measurable contribution context, not worker work (it is not dispatched as a worker job).
Add a line: PKRs hand back on unknown discovery instead of researching unknowns inside execution.

requirements.description: Accumulated anti-goals as a candidate guardrail library with governance fields.
requirements.hint: Define candidate-anti-goals.v1.json with metric_id, threshold, type, applies_when/does_not_apply_when, invalidates_when/recertify_by, candidate_status, no_regression_evidence, and ratification boundary.

requirements.description: Accumulated anti-goals are inputs not authority; the three zero-valued anti-goals stated.
requirements.hint: State the three governance anti-goals == 0 and that previous-run anti-goals are automatic inputs, not automatic authority.
Define all four flags: cannot, breaking, pointless, authority drift.
Show the flag lifecycle operationally: open/acknowledged/resolved/waived, each with an owner and a blocking status; breaking pauses committing moves.
Add an Operating Loop line: 'Heartbeat cadence and next_check_at:' with a time-based ten-minute heartbeat and next_check_at.
Note that long-running workers write file-based progress reports under .okra/workers/.
Include a metric freshness contract with observed_at and max_age/stale handling.

tokens: The orchestrator owns objective checks, check-ins, the OKR board, and subagent steering until the objective metric reaches target.

requirements.hint: Include the exact orchestrator-ownership sentence before any variants.
Instantiate the target as handoff_contract_acceptance_rate >= 0.90.
Include the three eval points: admissibility before action, direct read after action, paired goal/anti-goal eval.
Include a no-cascade direct-metric-read section.
Include the frame/tree run-store schema with frame_version, tree_version, orchestrator (objective checks + subagent steering), dkrs, ckrs, pkrs.
Include the human-only line: the human owns the frame; the loop raises evidence and the human decides.
